refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into data acquisition and data processing components, each contributing to total latency. This decomposition allows targeted design parameters to address each latency source.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure and verify the p95 latency of data acquisition and data processing separately, then confirm combined update latency meets ≤1.0 s requirement under supported scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-throughput telemetry data streams with prioritized scheduling
DP2: Implement real-time incremental model update algorithms with parallel processing
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address acquisition and processing latency respectively

potential_sources: Data acquisition delays impacting processing start time
Processing bottlenecks delaying model update completion
coupling_check.notes: DP1 and DP2 are logically decoupled but require interface synchronization; coupling is manageable with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes telemetry data quality and network link reliability meet minimum thresholds
Risk of processing algorithm complexity causing latency spikes under peak load
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor processing load and telemetry link performance; consider fallback or load shedding strategies if latency targets are at risk.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for integrating telemetry acquisition and model update processing

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time sensor and vehicle data streams for worksite updates

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between vehicles, teleoperation, and central processing

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes update logic and data acquisition, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
Limited containment; vehicle provides raw data but does not process updates.
Primarily tasking and control; minimal role in update latency.
Focused on safety alerts, not involved in update processing.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update latency functions within NGHE Solution module reduces interface complexity and latency diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded
Dependency on network link reliability for timely data acquisition
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate load distribution within NGHE Solution; consider modular subcomponents if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR decomposition into acquisition and processing latency is clear and logically mapped to DP selections addressing each sub-FR.

diffusion_risks: Risk of latency increase if NGHE Solution module interfaces are not optimized
Potential overload in NGHE Solution if processing and acquisition are not balanced
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: High containment score for NGHE Solution supports architectural clarity and ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency measurements can be performed end-to-end and at module interfaces using telemetry and network link data.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of update latency responsibility to NGHE Solution module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor NGHE Solution module load and interface performance
Consider fallback mechanisms for network link degradation

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and mapping to DP and IM is consistent, feasible, and meets architectural clarity requirements with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Telemetry and network link meet reliability and throughput requirements
Processing algorithms can be optimized for real-time performance
open_risks: Processing load spikes causing latency violations
evidence_needed: Latency performance data from telemetry and processing modules
Load testing results under peak operational scenarios
